relevant_files.role: Extension registry: EXTENSION_MAP constant and language_for_extension() function; supported_languages() slice
Language registration: LanguageInfo struct, get_language_info() match arms, get_ts_language() match arms
Template for Kotlin: ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, extract_inheritance() handler, test patterns
Feature flags: default feature set, lang-* feature definitions, optional dependencies
Workspace dependencies: tree-sitter-kotlin to be added to [workspace.dependencies]
Integration test patterns: tempfile::TempDir, analyze_directory(), file I/O

conventions.commits: Conventional Commits (feat, fix, docs, refactor, test, chore). Format: type(scope): subject. All commits GPG-signed with --signoff (DCO). Example: git commit -S --signoff -m 'feat(lang): add Kotlin grammar support'
conventions.testing: AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert). One happy path + one edge case per test behavior. Unit tests in language module under #[cfg(all(test, feature = 'lang-kotlin'))]. Integration tests in tests/integration_tests.rs. Helper function: parse_kotlin(src) -> tree_sitter::Tree
conventions.linting: cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo deny check advisories licenses. All required before PR submission.
conventions.error_handling: thiserror for error types. Result<T, E> return types. No unwrap() in production code paths. Errors propagated via ? operator.
conventions.module_organization: pub mod languages; in lib.rs. Each language in languages/<lang>.rs. Feature-gated module declarations in languages/mod.rs with #[cfg(feature = 'lang-*')].

patterns: Feature-gated language modules: #[cfg(feature = 'lang-kotlin')] gates module declaration, dependency, and all registration arms
Dual registration: (1) Extension mapping in lang.rs EXTENSION_MAP and supported_languages(); (2) Language info in languages/mod.rs get_language_info() and get_ts_language()
Query constants as &str: ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY exported as public constants; DEFUSE_QUERY optional
Optional handler functions: extract_inheritance() always present; extract_function_name, find_method_for_receiver, find_receiver_type set to None for Kotlin (no impl blocks)
Tree-sitter query syntax: S-expression format with @capture names; navigation via field_by_field_name() and node.kind()
Test helper pattern: parse_<lang>(src) -> tree_sitter::Tree using Parser::new(), set_language(), parse()
Inheritance extraction: walk delegation_specifiers children, distinguish superclass_type_with_constructor (has parens) from user_type (interfaces, no parens)

related_issues.title: feat(lang): add Kotlin grammar support
related_issues.relevance: Primary task. Specifies acceptance criteria (Cargo dependency, feature flag, query module, extension registry, language info, module registration, unit tests, linting). Defines node kinds and inheritance model.

related_issues.title: Reduce binary size by dropping unused language grammars
related_issues.relevance: Context: Kotlin was removed due to binary size (8.9 MB reduction) and lack of semantic handlers. Issue #649 re-adds with full semantic support.

related_issues.title: chore(languages): drop unused grammars (kotlin, swift, ruby, javascript)
related_issues.relevance: Removal PR (commit bc3c399). Shows minimal old Kotlin implementation (12 lines). Justifies re-implementation with full queries and handlers.

related_issues.title: feat(languages): add Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Go, Java, Kotlin, Swift, Ruby modules
related_issues.relevance: Original Kotlin addition (commit f734a42, 2026-03-02). Shows early design; old implementation used tree-sitter-kotlin-ng 1.1.0 (now tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8).

constraints: READ-ONLY: No code changes, no commits to target repo
Feature flag: lang-kotlin must be optional and added to default feature set in crates/code-analyze-core/Cargo.toml
Cargo dependency: tree-sitter-kotlin = '0.3.8' in root Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]
No impl_query, impl_trait_query, extract_function_name, find_method_for_receiver, find_receiver_type (Kotlin has no impl blocks; set to None)
No DEFUSE_QUERY (def-use analysis out of scope per issue #649; Fortran also omits)
All tests must pass: cargo test --features lang-kotlin
All clippy warnings must be resolved: cargo clippy -- -D warnings
SPDX header required: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2026 code-analyze-mcp contributors; SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
test_coverage: Existing languages (Rust, Python, Go, Java, TypeScript, JavaScript, Fortran, C++, C#) all have: (1) test_<lang>_element_query_happy_path() testing ELEMENT_QUERY compilation and node matching (class + method/function extraction); (2) test_<lang>_extract_inheritance() testing superclass and interface extraction; (3) edge case tests for uppercase extensions, unknown languages, etc.; (4) integration tests in tests/integration_tests.rs using tempfile::TempDir. Kotlin must follow identical pattern with 9 test cases per issue #649 acceptance criteria.

library_findings.relevant_api: tree_sitter_kotlin::LANGUAGE constant; node kinds: function_declaration, class_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_class_declaration, object_declaration, call_expression, delegation_specifiers, superclass_type_with_constructor, user_type
library_findings.notes: High reputation (79.1 benchmark score). Supports full Kotlin syntax including modifiers (data, companion), delegation specifiers for inheritance, enum entries with nested declarations. Query syntax is S-expression format compatible with tree_sitter::Query::new().

library_findings.relevant_api: Parser::new(), Parser::set_language(), Parser::parse(), Query::new(), QueryCursor::new(), Node::child_by_field_name(), Node::kind(), Node::start_byte(), Node::end_byte()
library_findings.notes: Core tree-sitter Rust bindings. Used to compile and execute queries, navigate syntax tree, extract source text by byte range.

approaches.name: Approach 1: Full semantic support with inheritance extraction (Recommended)
approaches.description: Implement complete Kotlin language support following issue #649 acceptance criteria. Add tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 to workspace dependencies. Create crates/code-analyze-core/src/languages/kotlin.rs with ELEMENT_QUERY (function_declaration, class_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_class_declaration, object_declaration), CALL_QUERY (call_expression), REFERENCE_QUERY (identifier references), IMPORT_QUERY (import_header), and extract_inheritance() handler that walks delegation_specifiers to distinguish superclass (superclass_type_with_constructor with parens) from interfaces (user_type without parens). Register .kt and .kts extensions in lang.rs EXTENSION_MAP. Add lang-kotlin feature flag to crates/code-analyze-core/Cargo.toml default features. Register 'kotlin' in get_language_info() and get_ts_language() in languages/mod.rs. Implement 9 unit tests (free function, method in class, function call, class declaration, data class, interface, enum class, import statement, inheritance). All tests pass with cargo test --features lang-kotlin.
pros: Matches existing language implementation patterns (Java, Python, Go)
Enables symbol extraction, call graph analysis, and inheritance tracking for Kotlin
Supports all Kotlin declaration types (data class, companion object, enum, interface)
Comprehensive test coverage (9 test cases) ensures correctness
Minimal code duplication; follows established patterns
cons: Requires careful tree-sitter-kotlin node kind understanding (delegation_specifiers complexity)
Larger implementation (~200 lines for kotlin.rs + tests)
Increases binary size by ~8.9 MB (trade-off accepted per issue #649 context)

approaches.name: Approach 2: Minimal semantic support (no inheritance extraction)
approaches.description: Add Kotlin support with basic queries only: ELEMENT_QUERY (function_declaration, class_declaration, object_declaration), CALL_QUERY (call_expression), REFERENCE_QUERY (identifier), IMPORT_QUERY (import_header). Omit extract_inheritance() handler (set to None in LanguageInfo). Register extensions and feature flag as in Approach 1. Implement 5 unit tests (function, class, call, import, unknown language edge case). Justification: Kotlin inheritance model (delegation_specifiers) is complex; defer extraction to future PR if demand arises.
pros: Simpler implementation (~100 lines for kotlin.rs + tests)
Covers core use cases: symbol extraction and call analysis
Reduces risk of inheritance extraction bugs
cons: Does not fully satisfy issue #649 acceptance criteria (inheritance extraction required)
Reduces value for Kotlin projects with complex class hierarchies
Inconsistent with other languages (Java, Python, Go all have inheritance extraction)
May require rework if issue is not closed as-is

approaches.name: Approach 3: Phased implementation with feature flag for inheritance
approaches.description: Add Kotlin support in two phases: Phase 1 (this PR) adds basic queries (ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY) with extract_inheritance() returning empty Vec. Phase 2 (follow-up PR) implements delegation_specifiers walking to populate inheritance. Register extensions and feature flag as in Approach 1. Implement 5 unit tests for Phase 1 (function, class, call, import, edge case); add 4 more tests in Phase 2 (single inheritance, multiple interfaces, mixed inheritance, companion object). Justification: Reduces scope and risk; allows incremental validation.
pros: Reduces initial implementation scope and risk
Enables Kotlin support to land faster (Phase 1)
Inheritance extraction can be validated separately (Phase 2)
cons: Does not fully satisfy issue #649 in single PR
Requires two PRs instead of one
Inheritance extraction deferred; may not be prioritized
Inconsistent with issue acceptance criteria (inheritance required)

recommendation: Approach 1 (Full semantic support with inheritance extraction). Rationale: Issue #649 explicitly requires inheritance extraction via extract_inheritance() handler in acceptance criteria. Approach 1 fully satisfies the issue, matches existing language patterns (Java, Python, Go), and provides maximum value for Kotlin codebases. The inheritance extraction logic is well-scoped: walk delegation_specifiers children, check for superclass_type_with_constructor (superclass with parens) vs user_type (interfaces without parens). Tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 provides robust node kinds and field names. Test coverage (9 cases) ensures correctness. Binary size trade-off (8.9 MB) is acceptable per issue context. Estimated complexity: medium (200 lines code + 300 lines tests). Risk: low (follows established patterns; tree-sitter-kotlin is mature).
